question.en: What can I encode in a QR code?

answer.en: You can encode URLs, plain text, email addresses, phone numbers, WiFi credentials, contact cards (vCard), geographic coordinates, and any other text data up to about 3000 characters.

question.en: What error correction level should I choose?

answer.en: L (7%) for digital-only use where the code is always clean. M (15%) is the default balance. Q (25%) for printed codes that may get slightly damaged. H (30%) for codes on products that may be scratched.

question.en: What size should I make the QR code?

answer.en: For on-screen display, 200–300px is fine. For printing on business cards, use at least 1 inch (300px at 300 DPI). Larger sizes allow lower-quality scans to still succeed.

question.en: Can I customize the colors of a QR code?

answer.en: Yes. You can change the dark and light colors. Ensure sufficient contrast for reliable scanning — the dark color should be significantly darker than the light color.
